response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack engineer whose career is built on TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and JavaScript — not C++. His C++ exposure is minimal: roughly 6 months at National Instruments (2007–2008) writing components for a C IDE, plus a brief bachelor thesis project. This falls drastically short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience, no fixed-income or derivatives domain knowledge, and no Windows-platform development history. His sole finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital, 2013–2014) involved a Node/TypeScript stack at a retail-investment startup, not capital-markets or fixed-income infrastructure.
strengths: 20+ years of broad software engineering experience across multiple languages and domains, demonstrating strong general fundamentals
Exposure to a financial-technology context at RIDE Capital (2013–2014), showing some familiarity with investment-related business requirements
Demonstrated low-latency and high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic, indicating performance-oriented thinking
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately 6 months at National Instruments in 2007–2008 and a thesis project, versus the required 5–10 years
No Windows-platform development history; primary runtime environment appears to be Linux throughout career
No SQL Server or Oracle experience and zero fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: No fixed-income or capital-markets domain expertise
